emails.subject: Quick Digital Backup Idea
emails.body:
One thing people often recommend before relocating internationally is making a backup of important digital files.

Things like photos, documents, contacts, and important files from your laptop or phone.

Sometimes it’s easier to organize these before traveling rather than after settling in.

emails.subject: Important Documents to Download Before Leaving
emails.body:
One thing many people recommend when moving countries is downloading important documents before leaving.

Things like:
• bank statements
• tax records
• employment documents

Sometimes it becomes harder to access those accounts later from another country, so it's useful to keep copies saved on your computer or cloud storage.

Just thought this might help before the move.

emails.subject: Final Checklist Before You Move
emails.body:
Download Important Documents
Before leaving Canada, download and save copies of important documents such as bank statements, tax records, insurance documents, and employment letters. Keep them in multiple places like your phone, laptop, USB drive, and cloud storage.

Inform Your Bank About Moving
Many banks flag or block accounts if they detect foreign logins. Inform your bank that you are relocating to India so that online access and transactions continue without issues.

Cancel Subscriptions
People often forget to cancel recurring payments. Check and cancel services such as streaming subscriptions, gym memberships, internet plans, and any automatic monthly payments.

Backup Phone Contacts
Ensure all contacts are backed up and synced so you don’t lose them if you change phones or SIM cards after arriving in India.

Download Important Emails
If you are leaving a job in Canada, download copies of important emails such as offer letters, experience certificates, payslips, and HR communication before your company email access is disabled.

Carry Power Adapters
Canada uses Type A/B plugs while India uses Type C/D/M. Bring a universal adapter or power strip so your devices work immediately after arrival.

Bring Prescription Medicines
Some medicines available in Canada may not be easily available in India. Carry prescriptions and bring a few months’ supply if needed.

Backup Laptop and Phone Data
Before traveling, backup photos, files, passwords, and documents from your devices to avoid losing important information.

Save Emergency Contacts
Save phone numbers for airlines, banks, family members, and important contacts both in your phone and somewhere offline.

Inform Credit Card Companies
Credit card companies sometimes block international transactions if they see activity from another country. Inform them about your relocation.

Close Unused Bank Accounts
Some Canadian banks charge monthly maintenance fees. Close any accounts you do not plan to use to avoid unnecessary charges.

Cancel Apartment Lease Properly
Ensure the lease termination notice is completed and the security deposit is returned. Also confirm that no pending payments remain.

Sell or Donate Large Items
Furniture, appliances, and other bulky items are expensive to ship internationally. Sell, donate, or give them away before leaving.

Backup Two-Factor Authentication
If you use authentication apps for banking or email security, ensure they remain accessible after changing your phone or SIM.

Download Offline Maps
Download offline maps of the destination city in India so you can navigate even if your internet connection isn’t working immediately after arrival.

Bring Hard-to-Find Items
Some items are more expensive or harder to find in India, such as high-quality winter jackets, certain electronics accessories, or specific skincare products.

Keep Some Canadian Dollars
Do not convert all currency immediately. Keep a small amount of Canadian dollars in case you need them during travel.

Pack Important Documents in Cabin Luggage
Never place critical documents such as passport, visa, certificates, or money in checked luggage. Always keep them in your carry-on bag.

Enable International Roaming
Enable roaming temporarily so your phone works when you land, at least until you get an Indian SIM card.

Plan the First Week in India
Before leaving Canada, think about the first few days in India—arrange accommodation, transportation from the airport, and getting a local SIM card and internet access.
